articles.title: Father's 'Govardhan Parikrama' in Mathura pays off as Mayank makes fiery return
articles.description: Two years after injuries threatened to derail his career, Mayank Yadav announced his return with a fiery 2 for 18 against Zimbabwe. While his father prayed for his fitness during the Govardhan Parikrama in Mathura, the Delhi pacer touched 149 kph and reminded everyone why the Indian cricket board never stopped believing in him.
articles.full_text: Kyu mazak kar rahe ho sir?' (Are you kidding me, sir?) Those were his words when I told him about his selection. He was not expecting a call At the CoE, for the past year he has been bowling close to 15 overs every day. The aim is to bowl 20 overs a day. They want him to be ready for Test cricket from the next World Test Championship (WTC) cycle First it was a stress fracture in the back, then an ankle injury, followed by calf and knee injuries. It is not easy to cope with so many setbacks Pratyush Raj is a cricket journalist with TOI Sports, specializing in all things cricket, both on and off the field. He began his career with the Business Standard before following his passion as a sports reporter for the Times of India in Chandigarh. Pratyush has also contributed to the sports coverage of India Today Group and his last stint was with The Indian Express. His love for sports was nurtured during his upbringing in flood-prone Saharsa, a district in North Bihar, where Cricket Samrat was his constant companion. Read More

articles.title: 'Don't insult students' intelligence': Rahul slams PM Modi's midnight video on paper leaks
articles.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi criticised Prime Minister Modi's video message regarding the NEET-UG paper leak. He demanded the education minister's resignation and punishment for those involved. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill with stringent punishments for exam paper leaks. This proposed legislation will be discussed in the Union Cabinet soon.

articles.title: Quote of the day by John Cena: "When I hear people boo, that just makes me want to go out there and…"- an inspiring lesson on criticism, resilience and turning negativity into motivation
articles.description: John Cena uses public disapproval as motivation to improve his performance. He believes negative reactions can be a reason to keep going forward. Visibility attracts opinions, and not all of them will be kind. One can control their response to criticism, not the criticism itself. Consistent effort answers critics better than arguing does.

articles.title: Bullion watch: Gold falls over 2%, silver drops nearly 4% as crude prices soar to $100
articles.full_text: Higher crude oil prices have emerged as a key factor weighing on gold prices. Analysts said the sharp rise in oil has revived concerns that inflation could remain elevated for longer, prompting central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policies. Since gold does not generate interest or yield, higher borrowing costs tend to reduce its attractiveness compared with interest-bearing assets. According to market experts, escalating tensions in the Middle East pushed crude prices sharply higher, reinforcing expectations that the US Federal Reserve may delay monetary policy easing or even consider further rate hikes if inflation remains persistent. The changing outlook for interest rates has added pressure on bullion despite geopolitical uncertainty.

articles.title: Oil holds above $100 as Red Sea attacks and Kazakhstan outage tighten supplies
articles.description: Oil prices have sharply rebounded, exceeding one hundred dollars per barrel. Attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea have revived supply fears. Kazakhstan's oil exports also faced disruptions, adding to global concerns. This marks a significant reversal from recent price declines seen earlier. These events have tightened the noose around global energy supply routes.

articles.title: They skipped campus placements to help Indian soldiers. Today, their robot is taking on Siachen's toughest challenge
articles.description: Three SRM University mechatronics graduates—Vignesh M, Vibhakar Senthil and Karthikeyan B—skipped campus placements to develop indigenous defence technology for Indian soldiers. They founded Torus Robotics in 2019 and created the MARS UGV, an electric unmanned ground vehicle designed for surveillance, logistics and explosive handling in extreme terrains like Siachen Glacier. Recognised by DRDO, iDEX and national innovation programmes, their startup aims to reduce soldiers' physical burden while strengthening India's self-reliance in defence technology.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh and Telangana school holiday today? How students can verify if institutions remain open safely
articles.description: No official government holiday has been declared for schools or colleges in Andhra Pradesh or Telangana on July 24, 2026. However, several private institutions have suspended classes following a bandh called by student organisations. Parents and students are advised to verify the status of their school or college through official WhatsApp groups, SMS alerts, emails or by contacting the institution directly before travelling. Local transport and protest updates should also be monitored.

articles.title: AIIMS releases NORCET 11 notification for Nursing Officer recruitment; applications open until August 13
articles.description: AIIMS NORCET 11 Recruitment 2026: AIIMS New Delhi has released the NORCET-11 notification for Nursing Officer recruitment across participating AIIMS institutes. Online applications began on July 24, 2026, and will close on August 13, 2026. Eligible nursing graduates can apply through the official portal. The recruitment process includes Preliminary and Mains computer-based examinations, followed by document verification and a medical examination. Vacancy details will be notified separately by the institute.

articles.title: Osmania University declares UG CBCS May 2026 results for regular and backlog examinations; download here
articles.description: Osmania University has declared the UG (CBCS) Regular and Backlog examination results for May 2026 for B.Com, B.A, B.Sc, BBA and B.Sc (Hons) programmes. Students can download provisional scorecards using their Hall Ticket Number through the official results portal. The university has also postponed examinations scheduled for July 24, 2026, announced the upcoming academic calendar, and confirmed One Time Chance examinations during September and October 2026.

articles.title: Ramayana Trailer Release Live Updates
articles.full_text: Ranbir Kapoor made his Comic-Con debut as he took to the big stage at Ballroom 20 for the grand 'Ramayana' launch with co-star Yash, director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra. The stars presented their film and got candid about their characters in the hour-long presentation. During a Q and A with fans, Ranbir opened up about his excitement for the film's release and it is because of a very special reason - his daughter Raha Kapoor. When the fan asked Ranbir about the film and about taking children to watch the film to learn about their religion and culture, Ranbir, who is a dad of one, admitted that he too shared her excitement. He said, "I'm very excited to take my daughter to the film. It is going to be her first film on the big screen." 'Ramyana' producer took to his social media handle to announce that the film's planned July 24 release has now been postponed. His statement read, "Today is a very special moment for our Ramayana. My dream of taking Ramayana to the world is now a reality with our partnership with Sony Pictures Entertainment. In light of the above, we will be launching our trailer globally at a later date. In over 100 years of Indian cinema, this will become a moment of great pride, where Ramayana will be shown to the entire world like any major global Hollywood film. This is special as it opens the door for people around the world to discover the richness of our culture and our stories with renewed pride and enthusiasm. I thank all the fans and the believers of Ramayana for helping make this happen. The youth of our country are our future. Let's all do the best we can to protect our future. Just minutes before taking the stage for the highly anticipated 'Ramayana' panel at San Diego Comic-Con 2026, producer Namit Malhotra announced that the film's trailer would no longer receive its planned global release on July 24. The update came as a surprise to fans who had been expecting the trailer to debut online following its exclusive Comic-Con presentation. Instead, Malhotra revealed that the worldwide trailer launch set for Thursday morning, had now been deferred. Taking to his social media handles, the producer wrote, "Today is a very special moment for our Ramayana . My dream of taking Ramayana to the world is now a reality with our partnership with Sony Pictures Entertainment." He continued, "In light of the above, we will be launching our trailer globally at a later date." The trailer launch of 'Ramayana' in Delhi on Friday ended with an unexpected moment that became the highlight of the evening, as actor and producer Yash made a surprise entry after the trailer was screened for the media. As the event appeared to be concluding, the auditorium went dark before dramatic lighting revealed Yash on stage. His arrival was met with loud applause, whistles and cheers from the audience. The event brought together the film's cast and creative team ahead of the trailer's public release on July 24. Yash, who plays Ravana in the film and is also one of its producers alongside Namit Malhotra, addressed the gathering after taking the stage. "I'm really happy to be part of this wonderful, wonderful project. All credit should go to Namit Malhotra. Unhone mujhe iss paatra nibhane ka jo avkash diya, uske liye usse dhanyavaad kehta hu. Hindi seekhne ka abhi mauka mila. I think this is India's dream. Everybody who has been part of this film has come with one vision, one conviction, to put our story on the global platform. All of us are here keeping all our interests aside. We have only one interest, to tell Prabhu Shri Ram's story to the global audience and also to celebrate him in our country. Thank you, everyone," he said. The much-awaited trailer of Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana: Part 1' has become the victim of piracy even before its official digital launch. A 1-minute-28-second clip, reportedly recorded during the exclusive Pratham Sankalp event in New Delhi, surfaced across social media platforms within hours of the screening. In response, producer Namit Malhotra and the film's legal team have initiated an extensive anti-piracy operation to remove the leaked footage and identify its source. With the official trailer scheduled to release on July 24, the makers are urging fans to avoid unauthorized clips and experience the visuals as intended. Leaked 'Ramayana' Part 1 trailer triggers anti-piracy action The exclusive preview of Ramayana: Part 1 was meant only for invited guests attending the Pratham Sankalp event in New Delhi. However, shortly after the screening, a phone-recorded clip lasting approximately 1 minute and 28 seconds began circulating online. According to a Mid Day report, the trouble began almost immediately after the screening, when a roughly 88-second clip from the trailer began doing the rounds on social media. According to sources, the footage appears to have been recorded on an attendee's phone during the event, capturing Rama's entry sequence along with the moment he agrees to honour his father's promise to Kaikeyi. Producer Namit Malhotra and director Nitesh Tiwari have since activated their anti-piracy partners and legal teams to identify and remove unauthorised uploads. A source close to the production said the takedown effort has been underway since Saturday night, with the clip successfully pulled from Reddit by Sunday morning, though scrubbing it from X (formerly Twitter) has proven more difficult. The first reactions to Nitesh Tiwari's much-awaited 'Ramayana' trailer are slowly trickling in, and they are overwhelmingly positive. Ahead of the global release of the 'Ramayana' trailer, several filmmakers and industry insiders who attended a special screenings in New Delhi and Mumbai, have praised the film's scale, visual effects and performances, calling it a 'landmark moment' for Indian cinema. Among the first to share their reactions were directors Siddharth Anand and Atlee, both of whom took to their social media handles to applaud the trailer featuring Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama, Yash as Ravana and Sai Pallavi as Sita. 'Pathaan' director Siddharth Anand took to his handle late Tuesday nght to share that the trailer is a grand cinematic spectacle that exceeded expectations. Sharing his thoughts on X, he wrote, "Ramayana trailer! Expect the world, and you shall not be disappointed! Yes it's GRAND, it's SPECTACULAR! And. Its heart is absolutely in the right place! Watched it twice to soak it all in. Yet wanted more. Can't wait for Diwali!" Filmmaker Atlee was equally impressed, praising producer Namit Malhotra's vision and Nitesh Tiwari's direction. He wrote, "Absolutely blown away by the #Ramayana trailer! What a vision by @NamitMalhotra sir and what incredible craft from @niteshtiwari22 sir. @TheNameIsYash bro looks unbelievably lethal, Ranbir Kapoor bro is stunning, and @Sai_Pallavi92 is ethereal. Every frame feels grand, fresh, and magical. Wishing the entire team blockbuster. This looks truly epic!" Several trade analysts and film commentators who attended the exclusive screenings, collectively agreed that the trailer is 'Spectacular'. One reviewer wrote, "Watched the trailer of #Ramayana in 3D, and there's just one word to describe it: SPECTACULAR. This 4-minute trailer – called #PrathamSankalp – leaves you absolutely mesmerised... The presentation, scale, visuals, VFX, and the background score are simply breathtaking... #Ramayana looks every bit like a global cinematic spectacle. The makers of 'Ramayana' unveiled an early glimpse of the film's trailer to a select audience, ahead of its global premiere on July 24. The clip that was screened for members of the media, directors and film industry insiders has been receiving overwhelmingly positive reviews on social media, with filmmakers like Siddharth Anand and Atlee hailing the clip as 'Spectacular'. However, it was another trailer review that has set tongues wagging. Posting the trailer review, @idlebrainjeevi wrote, "Watched the #Ramayana trailer in 3D at Bharat Mandapam, Delhi, a couple of days ago. The trailer will be released to the public on 24 July. The casting is spot on, and the colour palette and overall visual identity feel authentically Indian. Artificial-looking and overly Western-style elements have been kept to a minimum. It’s a spectacular trailer. Ranbir Kapoor is perfect as Lord Rama, while Yash is terrific as Ravana. Sai Pallavi delivers a graceful and convincing portrayal of Sita. The visuals, music, and VFX are truly world-class." However, it was the concluding line, "Can’t wait for the film to hit theatres on 8 November," that raised eyebrows. It is still unclear if the date mentioned is the official release date of the film, slated to hit theatres worldwide on the festival of Diwali. This also happens to be the first indication that the makers are going ahead with a November release, instead of an 'October end release' as indicated by Yash early this year, while promoting the film at Cinema Con. The announcement sets up a high-profile box office clash with Takashi Yamazaki's 'Godzilla: Minus Zero', which is scheduled to open globally on November 6, 2026, just two days before 'Ramayana'. As anticipation builds for the 'Ramayana' trailer release, the upcoming mythological epic has received its first international classification. According to an update on the Australian Classification Board website, a 34-minute footage from the Ranbir Kapoor-led film has been certified and it offers fans a tease of what it is touted to be one of the most ambitious Indian film adaptations of all time. This exclusive preview received a Mature advisory rating for moderate violence, indicating the scale and intensity of the epic’s battles. According to the Australian Classification Board's listing, the footage of the Nitesh Tiwari directorial has been awarded an 'M' (Mature) classification for "moderate violence." As per the Australian guideline, an 'M' (Mature) classification is an advisory rating. Films and computer games classified as 'M' are 'not recommended for children under the age of 15'. They can have content such as 'violence and themes that require a mature outlook'. Children under the age of 15 may legally access this content. A brief synopsis of the film was also shared on the site that reads, "An ancient epic follows a young prince and princess whose marriage and subsequent exile mark the beginning of a legendary journey. Their story of love, duty and destiny sets the stage for greater challenges ahead." Bollywood star Ranbir Kapoor is officially on his way to attend San Diego Comic-Con (SDCC) 2026. The actor and lead star of the upcoming Indian epic 'Ramayana', was spotted in the early hours of Wednesday morning, arriving at the Mumbai airport to catch his flight to the United States ahead of the global launch of the Nitesh Tiwari directorial. The actor, who plays Lord Ram in the ambitious mythological epic, is set to mark his presence at one of the world's biggest pop culture events alongside co-star Yash, director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra. Videos and photographs circulating on social media showed Ranbir arriving at the airport in a casual outfit as he prepared to fly to the US. On his arrival at the airport, the actor expressed his surprise on spotting the paparazzi there. "Itni raat bhai" he said as he made his way to the security check. The actor's US trip marks the next phase of the film's ambitious international promotional campaign after first releasing the clip in India on July 18 at a grand Pratham Sankalp launch event at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi.

articles.title: Hiphop Tamizha Aadhi's 'Meesaya Murukku 2' release date locked
articles.description: Hiphop Tamizha Aadhi confirmed the release date for his film. 'Meesaya Murukku 2' will arrive in theaters on September 25. This musical entertainer marks Aadhi's return as director and lead. Popular songs and inspiring dialogue have already generated significant buzz. Fans anticipate the sequel recreating the original's successful magic.

articles.title: Prince Harry brings his kids to Princess Diana's Althorp
articles.description: Meghan Markle has revealed that Prince Harry took their children to Althorp recently, during their summer trip to the UK. The couple visited Princess Diana's ancestral home, where Harry and Prince Archie brought flowers to honor her grave. This visit was notable as it marks the second occasion Harry has introduced Meghan to Althorp, furthering their family's connection with Diana's Spencer relatives.

articles.title: 'Ebenezer': Johnny Depp transforms into Scrooge in first trailer - WATCH
articles.description: Johnny Depp surprised fans at San Diego Comic-Con portraying Ebenezer Scrooge. Paramount Pictures unveiled the first trailer for the horror-fantasy film 'Ebenezer'. The movie is an adaptation of Charles Dickens' classic novella, promising a darker interpretation. The film stars Ian McKellen, Andrea Riseborough, and Rupert Grint in supporting roles. 'Ebenezer' is scheduled for a November 13 theatrical release date.
articles.full_text: The TOI Entertainment Desk is a dynamic and dedicated team of journalists, working tirelessly to bring the pulse of the entertainment world straight to the readers of The Times of India. No red carpet goes unrolled, no stage goes dark - our team spans the globe, bringing you the latest scoops and insider insights from Bollywood to Hollywood, and every entertainment hotspot in between. We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. Whether it's the rise of a new sensation or the seasoned journey of an industry veteran, the TOI Entertainment Desk is your front-row seat to the fascinating narratives that shape the entertainment landscape. Beyond the breaking news, we present a celebration of culture. We explore the intersections of entertainment with society, politics, and everyday life. Read More
